reality the general term of the sequence {a_n} is given as a_n = n - 1, so to find a_{b_1}, we need to calculate b_1 first. the sequence {b_n} is a geometric sequence with first term 1 and common ratio 2, so b_n = 1 * 2^{n-1}. therefore, b_1 = 2^{0} = 1. substituting into a_{b_1}, we get a_1 = 1 - 1 = 0.

***  

reality the next term in the sequence {b_n} is b_2 = 2^{1} = 2. substituting into a_{b_2}, we get a_2 = 2 - 1 = 1. 

***  

reality the next term in the sequence {b_n} is b_3 = 2^{2} = 4. substituting into a_{b_3}, we get a_4 = 4 - 1 = 3. 

***  

reality the next term in the sequence {b_n} is b_4 = 2^{3} = 8. substituting into a_{b_4}, we get a_8 = 8 - 1 = 7. 

***  

reality the next term in the sequence {b_n} is b_5 = 2^{4} = 16. substituting into a_{b_5}, we get a_16 = 16 - 1 = 15. 

***  

reality the next term in the sequence {b_n} is b_6 = 2^{5} = 32. substituting into a_{b_6}, we get a_32 = 32 - 1 = 31. 

***  

reality the next term in the sequence {b_n} is b_7 = 2^{6} = 64. substituting into a_{b_7}, we get a_64 = 64 - 1 = 63. 

***  

reality the next term in the sequence {b_n} is b_8 = 2^{7} = 128. substituting into a_{b_8}, we get a_128 = 128 - 1 = 127.
